title: VPN Concentrator Capacity
dataSources: SNMP OIDs, `cisco:asa` system events, vendor metrics API
spl:
index=snmp sourcetype="snmp:cpu" host="vpn-headend-*" earliest=-24h
| timechart span=15m avg(cpu_utilization) as avg_cpu by host
description: Tracks session count and CPU/memory against platform limits to avoid remote-access brownouts during peaks.
value: Network operations teams monitor VPN concentrator CPU, memory, and throughput against hardware limits, preventing resource exhaustion that causes connection failures before session license limits are reached.
implementation: Prefer vendor metrics (e.g., AnyConnect session count OID). Alert when CPU >80% sustained or sessions >85% of license. Simplify if only session logs: use UC-17.2.1 trend + license field.
visualization: Line chart (CPU vs sessions), Gauge (capacity %), Table (headends).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ SNMP data from VPN concentrators in `index=snmp` with `sourcetype=snmp:cpu` or `sourcetype=snmp:memory`. Alternatively, ASA syslog messages for resource utilization.
‚Ä¢ Key fields: `host`, `cpu_utilization`, `memory_utilization`, `active_sessions`.
‚Ä¢ Cisco ASA SNMP OIDs: cpmCPUTotal5minRev (.1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1.8), ciscoMemoryPoolFree (.1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1.6).
‚Ä¢ Create a `vpn_capacity.csv` lookup: `host,max_sessions,max_throughput_mbps,cpu_warning,cpu_critical,memory_warning`.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Verify SNMP data:
```spl
index=snmp sourcetype="snmp:cpu" host="vpn-*" earliest=-1h
| timechart span=5m avg(cpu_utilization) by host
```

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert

Primary search ‚Äî Concentrator resource utilization:
```spl
index=snmp sourcetype="snmp:cpu" host="vpn-*" earliest=-24h
| timechart span=15m avg(cpu_utilization) as avg_cpu max(cpu_utilization) as peak_cpu by host
| appendpipe [
    search index=snmp sourcetype="snmp:memory" host="vpn-*" earliest=-24h
    | timechart span=15m avg(memory_utilization) as avg_mem by host
]
```

Capacity scoring:
```spl
index=snmp host="vpn-*" earliest=-1h
| stats avg(cpu_utilization) as cpu avg(memory_utilization) as mem by host
| lookup vpn_capacity.csv host OUTPUT max_sessions cpu_warning cpu_critical memory_warning
| eval status=case(cpu>=cpu_critical, "CRITICAL", cpu>=cpu_warning OR mem>=memory_warning, "WARNING", 1==1, "OK")
```

Understanding this SPL: Monitors the hardware health of VPN concentrators. CPU spikes above 80% on ASA typically cause session establishment delays. Memory exhaustion causes connection drops. These metrics complement session-count monitoring (UC-17.2.1) ‚Äî a concentrator can have available session licenses but still be overloaded in CPU or memory.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
(a) On ASA: `show cpu usage` and `show memory`. Compare with Splunk.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Dashboard: Gauge tiles for CPU/memory per concentrator. Timechart trending.
Alert: Warning at 70% CPU, critical at 85%.

Runbook:
1. **High CPU** ‚Äî Check for DDoS, excessive logging, or crypto offload overload. Consider adding a second concentrator.
2. **High memory** ‚Äî Check for AnyConnect image cache, large ACL lists, or session leaks.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **No SNMP data** ‚Äî SNMP community string may be wrong, or ACL blocking the poller.
‚Ä¢ **CPU spikes during rekeying** ‚Äî Normal for large IPSec deployments. Schedule rekeying off-peak.
grandmaExplanation: We check the health of the machines that handle all our remote connections, making sure they have enough processing power and memory to handle everyone without slowing down.
